name: ExtensionTask_RequestedPeriod
title: R5: When the task should be performed (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Task.requestedPeriod` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Task.requestedPeriod` 0..1 `Period`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Task.requestedPeriod` 0..1 `Period`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Task.requestedPeriod` has a context of Task based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Task`.
Element `Task.requestedPeriod` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: When the task should be performed (new)
element.comment:
Element `Task.requestedPeriod` has a context of Task based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Task`.
Element `Task.requestedPeriod` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This is typically used when the Task is *not* seeking fulfillment of a focus Request, as in that case the period would be specified on the Request and/or in the Task.restriction.period.  Instead, it is used for stand-alone tasks.

element.short: When the task should be performed
element.definition: Indicates the start and/or end of the period of time when completion of the task is desired to take place.
element.comment: This is typically used when the Task is *not* seeking fulfillment of a focus Request, as in that case the period would be specified on the Request and/or in the Task.restriction.period.  Instead, it is used for stand-alone tasks.
